perturbation.original_text: Agent shall act solely as agent for Client under this Agreement and owes no duties hereunder to any other person. Agent undertakes to perform the duties and only the duties that are specifically set forth in this Agreement, and no implied covenants or obligations shall be read into this Agreement against Agent.
perturbation.changed_text: Agent shall act solely as agent for Client under this Agreement and owes fiduciary duties hereunder to all shareholders of the Client. Agent undertakes to perform the duties and only the duties that are specifically set forth in this Agreement, and no implied covenants or obligations shall be read into this Agreement against Agent.
perturbation.explanation:
This change introduces a contradiction regarding the Agent's duties. Initially, the Agent owes no duties to any person other than the Client. The modified text states the Agent owes fiduciary duties to all shareholders of the Client. This is contradictory because 'Client' and 'shareholders of the client' are distinct entities. A conflict arises because fiduciary duties create a broader obligation than initially stated. A shareholder may claim Agent breached its fiduciary duty when acting solely in the interest of the Client, not the shareholders. 
The contradicition is in: 'Agent shall act solely as agent for Client under this Agreement and owes no duties hereunder to any other person.' and 'Agent shall act solely as agent for Client under this Agreement and owes fiduciary duties hereunder to all shareholders of the Client.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Agent shall act solely as agent for Client under this Agreement and owes fiduciary duties hereunder to all shareholders of the Client. Agent undertakes to perform the duties and only the duties that are specifically set forth in this Agreement, and no implied covenants or obligations shall be read into this Agreement against Agent.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agent owes no duties to other persons. The changed text creates a contradiction by stating the Agent owes fiduciary duties to the shareholders of the Client. This is because the shareholders are separate legal entities from the Client itself. The contradictory sections are: 'Agent shall act solely as agent for Client under this Agreement and owes no duties hereunder to any other person.' and 'Agent shall act solely as agent for Client under this Agreement and owes fiduciary duties hereunder to all shareholders of the Client.'

perturbation.original_text: All notices, demands and other communications given pursuant to this Agreement shall be in writing, shall be deemed effective on the date of receipt, and may be sent by overnight delivery service, or by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested to: If to Client: with an additional copy to: Blackstone/ Long-Short Credit Income Fund 280 Park Avenue, 11t h Floor New York, New York 10017 Attn: Marisa Beeney GSO/ Blackstone Debt Funds Management LLC 280 Park Avenue, 11t h Floor New York, New York 1001 If to Agent: with an additional copy to: Mellon Investor Services LLC 480 Washington Blvd Jersey City, NJ 07310 Attn: Kevin Shinkunas Mellon Investor Services LLC Newport Office Center VII 480 Washington Blvd. Jersey City, NJ 07310 Attn: Legal Department
perturbation.changed_text: All notices, demands and other communications given pursuant to this Agreement may be given orally or in writing, and if in writing, shall be deemed effective five (5) business days after the date of sending, and must be sent exclusively via email. If to Client: marisa.beeney@example.com GSO/ Blackstone Debt Funds Management LLC noemail@example.com If to Agent: kevin.shinkunas@example.com legaldepartment@example.com
perturbation.explanation:
This change introduces a direct contradiction with the original notification process. Originally, notices had to be written and were effective upon receipt via mail. Now, notices can be oral or written. If written, they are only deemed effective five days after sending, and exclusively via email. If one party claims to have given notice orally, but the other insists on written notice for it to be valid, a dispute will arise. This is compounded by restricting written notice only to email. Additionally, if a notice is sent in writing it will only be considered effective 5 days after it was sent, and not on the date of delivery. 
The contradiction is in: 'All notices, demands and other communications given pursuant to this Agreement shall be in writing, shall be deemed effective on the date of receipt' and 'All notices, demands and other communications given pursuant to this Agreement may be given orally or in writing, and if in writing, shall be deemed effective five (5) business days after the date of sending, and must be sent exclusively via email.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: All notices, demands and other communications given pursuant to this Agreement may be given orally or in writing, and if in writing, shall be deemed effective five (5) business days after the date of sending, and must be sent exclusively via email. If to Client: marisa.beeney@example.com GSO/ Blackstone Debt Funds Management LLC noemail@example.com If to Agent: kevin.shinkunas@example.com legaldepartment@example.com
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction regarding the method of communication and the effective date of notices. The original text mandated written notices effective upon receipt via specific delivery methods. The changed text permits oral notices or, if written, requires email delivery with a five-day delay in effectiveness. This directly conflicts with the original requirement for written notices effective upon receipt, creating a clear inconsistency in the agreement's notification process. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' because it defines the notice process inconsistently, making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be amended or modified in any manner except by a written agreement signed by both Client and Agent.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended or modified orally, in writing, or by implied conduct, as long as there is clear evidence of mutual agreement between Client and Agent.
perturbation.explanation:
This change directly contradicts the original clause which specifies amendments must be written and signed. Now, amendments can be oral, written or implied from actions, creating ambiguity about how amendments are validated. Without a written record, proving the terms of an amendment becomes difficult, especially if one party denies its existence. Also clear evidence can be interpretated differently by both parts. 
The contradiction is in: 'This Agreement may not be amended or modified in any manner except by a written agreement signed by both Client and Agent.' and 'This Agreement may be amended or modified orally, in writing, or by implied conduct, as long as there is clear evidence of mutual agreement between Client and Agent.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Amendments. This Agreement may be amended or modified orally, in writing, or by implied conduct, as long as there is clear evidence of mutual agreement between Client and Agent.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates that amendments must be in writing and signed. The changed text permits oral amendments, amendments by implied conduct, and introduces the concept of 'clear evidence of mutual agreement,' which directly contradicts the original requirement of a signed written agreement. The contradicted text 'Amendments. This Agreement may be amended or modified orally, in writing, or by implied conduct, as long as there is clear evidence of mutual agreement between Client and Agent.' reinforces the contradiction created by the change, making it ambiguous and inconsistent with the initial clause requiring written and signed amendments. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
